paper_metadata.title: The public acceptance of green taxes: 2 million voters express their opinion
paper_metadata.empirical_scope_or_sample: 990-respondent post-referendum VOX survey of the 24 Sept 2000 Swiss federal vote; 2,016,973 actual voters on the energy-tax proposals
paper_metadata.carbon_tax_relevance_summary: Direct referendum evidence on public support for Swiss taxes on non-renewable/fossil energy, including a green tax reform that recycled revenues through lower social security contributions.

determinants.definition: Affinity with left-of-center or green parties.
determinants.mechanism: Signals pro-regulation and pro-environment values; also correlates with greater turnout in the referendum.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss 2000 referendum on three energy taxes, especially the green tax reform.
determinants.outcome_variable: approval of the energy-tax proposals / green tax reform vote
determinants.evidence_quote: voters who expressed greater affinity with a left-of-center or ecological party voted ... 35% to 45% in favor
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Marginal effect on approval: +0.3501*** for green tax reform.
determinants.notes: Strongest political determinant; also raises participation.

determinants.mechanism: May improve understanding of green taxes and raise patience for long-run environmental benefits.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss 2000 referendum on the three energy-tax proposals.
determinants.evidence_quote: voters with a high school or university degree ... voted significantly more in favor
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Marginal effects: +0.2669*** to +0.2858*** for green tax reform; positive for all three proposals.

determinants.mechanism: Older citizens were more hostile to the proposed energy taxes, possibly reflecting stronger cost concerns or lower support for new intervention.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss 2000 referendum on the three energy-tax proposals.
determinants.evidence_quote: the elderly voters ... were more hostile to the proposals in proportions of 12 to 17%
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Negative for green tax reform and solar initiative; weaker for energy conservation package.

determinants.definition: Household car ownership, measured as number of cars.
determinants.mechanism: Car owners expect higher fuel-price burdens from energy taxes, especially the larger green tax reform.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss 2000 referendum on energy taxes on fossil fuels.
determinants.evidence_quote: voters with cars were markedly less favorable ... the proportion of approval drops by 21% per car
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Green tax reform marginal effect: -0.2109***; weaker or absent for the other proposals.

determinants.definition: Residence in a municipality with 50,000+ inhabitants.
determinants.mechanism: Urban residents may perceive more pollution, congestion, and traffic noise, making energy taxes more attractive.
determinants.evidence_quote: citizens living in the largest municipalities ... were more favorable to the energy tax proposals
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Positive in several models, especially energy conservation package; smaller or less stable for the solar initiative.

determinants.mechanism: Likely reflects regional preferences, including stronger attachment to hydropower and subsidies embedded in one proposal.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Energy conservation package in the Swiss 2000 referendum.
determinants.outcome_variable: approval of the energy conservation package
determinants.evidence_quote: French-speaking voters ... were markedly more favorable to that proposal
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Marginal effect about +0.19 to +0.21 for the energy conservation package.
determinants.notes: Specific to the energy conservation package.

determinants.raw_name_in_paper: Priority government intervention vs. competition (1 = competition, 0 = regulation)
determinants.definition: Preference for government intervention/regulation over competition.
determinants.mechanism: Pro-regulation voters are more comfortable with state use of energy taxes to correct market prices.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss 2000 referendum on the three energy-tax proposals.
determinants.evidence_quote: the few voters ... with greater faith in government regulation ... were more favorable
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Green tax reform marginal effect for competition-coded variable: -0.2426** (i.e., pro-regulation voters more supportive).

determinants.raw_name_in_paper: Concern with income inequality (1 = at least some, 0 = no or little concern)
determinants.mechanism: Taxs perceived as regressive or unfair become less acceptable when distributive concerns are salient.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss 2000 referendum on the energy-tax proposals, especially the green tax reform.
determinants.evidence_quote: Concern with income inequality lowered the likelihood of accepting the taxes by some 12% to 17%
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Negative and significant for green tax reform; also negative for energy conservation package and solar initiative.

determinants.raw_name_in_paper: Concern with unemployment (1 = at least some, 0 = no or little concern)
determinants.mechanism: Employment-risk concerns reduce support for energy taxes viewed as potentially costly for jobs.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Green tax reform with lower social security contributions in the Swiss 2000 referendum.
determinants.outcome_variable: approval of the green tax reform
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Negative in Table 6, but author says the effect was not convincing overall.

determinants.raw_name_in_paper: Higher tax rate / much higher tax rate of the green tax reform
determinants.definition: Larger tax rate and resulting price increase.
determinants.mechanism: Higher tax burdens make support harder, especially for taxpayers with greater fuel exposure.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Comparison of the green tax reform with the lower-rate energy conservation package and solar initiative.
determinants.evidence_quote: The green tax reform provided for the highest tax rate by far
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Mainly mattered for multiple-car owners; not decisive for most voters.

determinants.raw_name_in_paper: Mode of revenue recycling / subsidies for renewable energy and energy conservation
determinants.definition: Revenue earmarking to renewable-energy and energy-conservation subsidies rather than payroll-based refunds.
determinants.mechanism: Some voters may prefer visible subsidized environmental uses, but the paper finds no clear fairness advantage overall.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Energy conservation package and solar initiative in the Swiss 2000 referendum.
determinants.outcome_variable: approval of the energy conservation package / solar initiative
determinants.evidence_quote: The energy conservation package earned the best score ... subsidies to renewable energies and energy conservation
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Best-performing proposal, but effect is confounded with lower tax rate and hydropower support.

exclusions_or_uncertainties.reason: Paper says income is not a clear-cut determinant; vote effects are not robust/significant enough for extraction as a main determinant.
Women participated less, but no proven difference in vote choice; author says gender played no role per se on outcomes.

exclusions_or_uncertainties.issue: Environmental concern / priority wealth vs environment
exclusions_or_uncertainties.reason: Conceptually relevant, but the paper notes it was omitted from the main model because it is tautological with the vote outcome.

exclusions_or_uncertainties.issue: Employment dividend / lower social security contributions
exclusions_or_uncertainties.reason: Discussed as a revenue-recycling hypothesis, but the paper reports it was not understood or valued and does not isolate a clear positive effect.
